forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: fc80
+project: builtin-commands
+title: Fix failing meta_tree_id_param_is_required_where_expected (unregister.id required flag is false)
+---
+## Problem
+
+`cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service --test meta_tree` fails on `meta_tree_id_param_is_required_where_expected`:
+
+```
+assertion `left == right` failed: unregister.id required flag
+  left: Bool(false)
+ right: true
+```
+
+The test (`crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/meta_tree.rs`) asserts that the `unregister`/`schema`/`execute`/`available` command verbs each expose `id` as a REQUIRED string param in the `io.swissarmyhammer/operations` `_meta` tree produced by `generate_operations_meta(operations())`. The `unregister` verb's `id` param currently surfaces `required: false`.
+
+This is a PRE-EXISTING failure, confirmed independent of the `applies_to`/field-gate work (task `2z8zavn`): stashing all of that task's changes and re-running `--test meta_tree` reproduces the identical failure on a clean tree. It is unrelated to `applies_to` / field gating — it concerns command-verb operation metadata (`operations()` / `generate_operations_meta`).
+
+## What
+
+Trace why the `unregister` op's `id` parameter is emitted with `required: false` and restore it to `required: true` (or update the test if the metadata change was intentional — but `schema`/`execute`/`available` all still report `required: true`, so `unregister` is the odd one out and most likely the regression). Look at `operations()` in `swissarmyhammer-command-service` and the operation-schema generation in `swissarmyhammer-operations`.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service --test meta_tree` passes (all 7 tests green).
+- [ ] `unregister.id` (and `schema`/`execute`/`available`.id) surface `required: true` in the meta tree.
+- [ ] Root cause understood and documented in the fix (was the required flag dropped, or the test stale?).
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — the failing test already exists and is red; make it green by fixing the metadata source. #bug #commands #command-service
\ No newline at end of file
